headlineSummary: Anthropic and OpenAI both drew negative sentiment with a close tie on limited evidence, while Microsoft Copilot was slightly negative on limited evidence [E1] [E4] [E5] [E6].

ranking.summary: Anthropic leaned negative on trust and strategy, with one positive defense of its model quality but stronger criticism that its PRs were hard to trust and its positioning was overhyped; the close tie with OpenAI is based on limited evidence [E3] [E4] [E5].

OpenAI skewed negative overall, as criticism that GPT-5.5 hallucinated more outweighed a positive benchmark accuracy comment, and it remains in a close tie with Anthropic on limited evidence [E1] [E2].
Microsoft Copilot was mildly negative on limited evidence, with one comment calling it a bad solution despite praising Microsoft’s dogfooding approach [E6].

evidence.excerpt: GPT-5.5 hallucinates 3x more than MIT-licensed GLM-5.2

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names GPT-5.5 and frames it as substantially worse on hallucination rate, which is strong negative model-quality evidence for OpenAI.

evidence.excerpt: Artificial Analysis says GPT-5.5 xhigh scores highest on AA-Omniscience accuracy.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly praises GPT-5.5 for leading the benchmark on accuracy, a positive model-quality claim about OpenAI's model.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Fable 5 was restricted by the US government just three days after its release, marking the first US AI ban stemming from national security. One of the biggest models in the world was banned because a single jailbreak was too much of a risk. Such a weird thing to start with. The legal status of Fable does not mean that it's not intelligent. If anything, the problem is the opposite, someone thinks it's too intelligent

annotations.rationale: By arguing that Claude Fable 5's restriction does not imply weak intelligence and instead suggests it is 'too intelligent,' the commenter defends and praises Anthropic's model quality.

evidence.excerpt: And for me 1800 files change PRs created by Anthropic overseen by one person is not necessarily adding to the package. Even it that'd be the best code and design in the world, I won't use it. I don't trust it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says PRs 'created by Anthropic' reduce trust and concludes 'I won't use it. I don't trust it,' which is strong negative trust sentiment toward Anthropic.

evidence.excerpt: it's overhyped technology and that Anthropic painted themselves into that corner by pretending it's as dangerous as nukes.

annotations.rationale: This directly criticizes Anthropic's positioning as hype-driven and self-defeating, expressing strong negative sentiment about the company's strategy and credibility.

evidence.excerpt: I kinda have to hand it to Microsoft for dogfooding vibecoding with Copilot to such an extent. You can't say they encourage their customers to use a bad solution while doing something different in-house.

annotations.rationale: The sarcastic remark explicitly names Copilot and calls it 'a bad solution,' which is negative sentiment toward Microsoft Copilot's usefulness/quality.
